commonQuestions.question: What is Binance?
commonQuestions.answer: The world's largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume, founded in 2017 by Changpeng Zhao (CZ), now led by CEO Richard Teng. Serving 280M+ users in 180+ countries with spot, futures, P2P, earn, copy trading, and Web3 products.

commonQuestions.question: Is Binance safe?
commonQuestions.answer: Binance is a top-tier centralized exchange. CoinGecko trust score: 10/10 (rank #2). Security context: 2019 hack — 7,000 BTC stolen, all users reimbursed via SAFU fund Like all CEXs, funds are custodied by the exchange — use hardware wallets for long-term storage. Enable 2FA, use a strong unique password, and whitelist withdrawal addresses where available. Never share API keys.

commonQuestions.question: What is the Binance promo/referral code?
commonQuestions.answer: The CryptoBonusWorld referral code for Binance is CRYPTOBONW. It is pre-filled automatically when you register via our link. Using it activates the welcome bonus of up to 19800 USDT.

commonQuestions.question: How much is the Binance welcome bonus?
commonQuestions.answer: Via CryptoBonusWorld, the current Binance welcome bonus is up to 19800 USDT. This is a task-based reward: completing KYC, making a qualifying deposit, and reaching trading volume milestones unlocks different reward tiers. The full amount requires all milestones — most users completing a standard deposit earn a meaningful first-week reward. Bonus amounts are typically trading fee vouchers, not withdrawable cash.

commonQuestions.question: How do I claim the Binance bonus?
commonQuestions.answer: Step 1: Click the registration link on the CryptoBonusWorld Binance page — the referral code/link is pre-filled. Step 2: Complete email/phone verification. Step 3: Complete KYC identity verification (required for bonuses). Step 4: Make a qualifying deposit (crypto transfer or card). Step 5: Check your Rewards Center / Bonus Hub for active tasks and complete trading volume milestones within the campaign window (usually 30 days). Vouchers are credited to your account as you complete each task tier.

commonQuestions.question: What are Binance spot trading fees?
commonQuestions.answer: Binance spot trading fees: 0.1% maker / 0.1% taker (standard). Using BNB for fee payment gives a 25% discount — reducing standard fees to 0.075% maker/taker.

commonQuestions.question: What are Binance futures trading fees?
commonQuestions.answer: Binance USDT-margined perpetual futures: 0.02% maker / 0.05% taker. Maximum leverage: 125×. Futures trading carries substantial risk of capital loss from liquidation. Up to 125× leverage available.

commonQuestions.question: Does Binance have P2P trading?
commonQuestions.answer: Binance P2P offers 0% platform fee with 100+ fiat currencies and 700+ payment methods. Binance escrow holds seller crypto before buyer sends fiat. Available at p2p.binance.com.

commonQuestions.question: Does Binance have copy trading?
commonQuestions.answer: Yes — Binance offers copy trading for spot and futures: users can follow qualified lead traders and automatically replicate their positions.

commonQuestions.question: Is Binance available in the United States?
commonQuestions.answer: No. Binance.com does not accept US residents. US users must use Binance.US (a separate regulated entity) — which has significantly fewer features and trading pairs than Binance.com.

commonQuestions.question: Is Binance available in the United Kingdom?
commonQuestions.answer: No. Binance.com is not available to UK residents following FCA action in 2021.

commonQuestions.question: What cryptocurrencies does Binance support?
commonQuestions.answer: Binance lists 430 cryptocurrencies across 1368 trading pairs. Binance regularly lists emerging altcoins alongside major assets.

commonQuestions.question: Does Binance have a mobile app?
commonQuestions.answer: Yes. Binance has official iOS (App Store) and Android (Google Play) apps. Download from the official Binance website (https://www.binance.com/app or equivalent) or directly from the official app store listing to avoid counterfeit apps. Note: the app may not be available in US/UK app stores.

commonQuestions.question: Does Binance require KYC?
commonQuestions.answer: Yes. Binance requires KYC (identity verification) to unlock full trading, withdrawals, and welcome bonus eligibility. Process: government-issued ID + selfie/liveness check. Typically completes in 2–30 minutes for automated approval; up to 24 hours for manual review.

commonQuestions.question: Does Binance have Proof of Reserves?
commonQuestions.answer: Yes. Binance publishes monthly Proof of Reserves via Merkle tree verification. Users can verify their specific balance is included. Historically audited by Mazars.

commonQuestions.question: What leverage does Binance offer?
commonQuestions.answer: Binance offers up to 125× leverage on futures. Standard: 10–20× for most trading pairs. High leverage available — suitable only for experienced traders with strict risk management. Leverage availability varies by asset and KYC level.

commonQuestions.question: When was Binance founded and who founded it?
commonQuestions.answer: Binance was founded in 2017. Founded by Changpeng Zhao (CZ, 2017–2023); current CEO Richard Teng (2023–present). Security history: 2019 hack — 7,000 BTC stolen, all users reimbursed via SAFU fund

commonQuestions.question: How many users does Binance have?
commonQuestions.answer: Binance serves 280M+ registered users across 180+ countries as of 2026.

commonQuestions.question: What is Binance's 24-hour trading volume?
commonQuestions.answer: Binance's 24-hour spot trading volume is approximately $8.31B (131 448,76 BTC) as of the last data pull (2026-06-08). This places Binance at CoinGecko rank #2 by trust-adjusted volume.

commonQuestions.question: Does Binance have a native token?
commonQuestions.answer: Yes. Binance's native token is BNB. Use: Trading fee discount (25% off), Launchpad allocation, Earn products, Gas on BNB Chain.

commonQuestions.question: What is Binance's CoinGecko trust score?
commonQuestions.answer: Binance has a CoinGecko trust score of 10/10, placing it at rank #2 globally. CoinGecko's trust score assesses exchange API accuracy, trading volume legitimacy, and cybersecurity posture.
